brief_justification: Paper A is an equilibrium statistical-mechanics problem: a thermodynamic phase transition (Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless / BEC critical point) in a trapped 2D interacting Bose gas, analyzed via mean-field Hartree-Fock plus the BKT prediction under a local-density approximation. Paper B is a driven nonlinear quantum-optics problem: parametric-oscillator dynamics of a strongly-driven two-level system with broken inversion symmetry, producing Rabi-frequency harmonics and parametric amplification. There is no common governing process or role-for-role correspondence — a temperature-driven critical threshold versus field-driven parametric gain are different machinery, so the only overlap is generic 'two-level/two-dimensional quantum system' vocabulary.
